Active Work Architecture
─[ Ceph Operations ]─ ┐

Ceph Operations

Purpose

Manage and troubleshoot Ceph storage cluster in Proxmox environment.

When to Use

  • Diagnosing Ceph health warnings or errors.
  • Managing pools, OSDs, or MDS daemons.
  • Planning storage capacity or performance changes.

Cluster Architecture

ComponentNodesNotes
MON[node-list]Quorum requires 2/3
MGR[node-list]Active + standby
OSD[osd-list]Storage devices
MDS[node-list]CephFS metadata

Network Layout

Refer to docs/knowledge/environment/ for subnet and VLAN assignments.

  • Ceph Public: Client + inter-daemon traffic.
  • Ceph Cluster: OSD replication traffic.

Health Monitoring

ceph -s                    # Overall cluster health
ceph health detail         # Detailed health messages
ceph osd tree              # OSD topology and status
ceph df                    # Pool usage statistics

Common Operations

Pool Management

  • List Pools: ceph osd pool ls detail
  • Create Pool: ceph osd pool create <name> <pg_num>
  • Enable App: ceph osd pool application enable <pool> <rbd|cephfs>

OSD Management

  • Mark OSD Out: ceph osd out <id> (Graceful removal)
  • Mark OSD In: ceph osd in <id> (Rejoin cluster)
  • Remove OSD: ceph osd crush remove osd.<id> -> ceph auth del osd.<id> -> ceph osd rm <id>

MDS Management

  • Add MDS to Node: pveceph mds create
  • Failover Test: systemctl stop ceph-mds@<name> on active node.

Troubleshooting

Cluster Won’t Start

  1. Check MON quorum: ceph mon stat.
  2. Verify MON connectivity on cluster network.
  3. Verify time sync (NTP) — Ceph is time-sensitive.

Slow Performance

  1. Check OSD latency: ceph osd perf.
  2. Check for recovery in progress: ceph -s.
  3. Check for full OSDs: ceph osd df.

Safety Rules

  • Never remove the last replica of data.
  • Always wait for recovery to complete before removing OSDs.
  • Keep at least 20% free space in cluster.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
